mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved profit expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 339299M as of March 2005 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive forward returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates tightening labor markets and accelerating wage growth, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate labor costs. This dual effect produces negative equity returns as margin compression dominates over the 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.2% as of March 2005 is near cyclical lows
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 5.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE and forward returns exceeds -0.05 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 2.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and increases refinancing costs for corporations. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the cumulative impact of higher borrowing costs materializes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.63% as of March 2005 is in tightening zone
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates when supply-side capacity utilization rises, signaling pricing power and margin expansion. Sustained INDPRO growth above trend produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.527 (2017=100) as of March 2005 reflects post-2001 recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any rolling 1-year window, or positive INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 5-day returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, the interaction indicates robust consumer and construction sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2195K and RSAFS at 339299M as of Feb-Mar 2005
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS grow month-over-month, S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K per month while UNRATE remains stable, signaling labor market softening without immediate wage pressure. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate future Fed rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100K and UNRATE stable within 0.2pp
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 100K and UNRATE stable, S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.0%, creating a real rate compression that favors equities over bonds. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real yields compress.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 192.0 and FEDFUNDS at 2.63% as of Feb-Mar 2005
failure_condition: When CPI > 2.5% YoY and FEDFUNDS < 3.0%, S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the real rate compression signal falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) remain elevated, signaling demand weakness masked by housing strength. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 10994.3B as of Q4 2004, HOUST at 2195K as of Feb 2005
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth < 2.0% and HOUST > 2000K, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC of the divergence signal exceeds -0.02 in absolute value.
